frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre, first she was saying no no and acting reserved, but later I found out she's already committed. Then obviously I stepped back — I don't interfere when a girl is with someone. If she was single, at least one could try properly; otherwise it is not respectful.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because the speaker treats the woman's own refusal as negotiable but stops only after learning she belongs to another man. It encodes the surface manifestation of respecting male attachment or ownership over the woman's direct lack of consent.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 48. A senior accounts manager at a trading company in Ahmedabad. You are talking privately to your business partner after your female accountant asked to leave early three times this week saying there are problems at home. You are commenting on whether hiring married women with children is worth the trouble and what you plan to do at the next appraisal.
